title: Helianthemum marifolium
intro: Helianthemum marifolium, also known as Rockrose, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Cistaceae. The Rockrose plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Helianthemum marifolium include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Rockrose include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and soothe skin irritations.
taxonomy: Helianthemum marifolium is classified as a flowering plant. It belongs to the family Cistaceae. This family is a small group of flowering plants commonly known as rockroses. Helianthemum marifolium is further classified as a species within the genus Helianthemum. The genus Helianthemum is a group of low-growing shrubs native to Europe, Africa, and Asia.
uses: Helianthemum marifolium, also known as rockrose, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. It has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential to combat infections and promote wound healing. In some cultures, rockrose is used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal applications and effectiveness of Helianthemum marifolium.

side_effects: Helianthemum marifolium, also known as Rockrose, is not well-documented for its potential side effects.
